output.title: Landlord Withholding Your Security Deposit? Here's What to Do Next
output.meta_description: Landlord withholding your security deposit? Learn your tenant rights and send a legally sound demand letter in 60 seconds—no signup needed.
content_outline: Is Your Landlord Withholding Your Security Deposit? Start Here
What Can a Landlord Legally Deduct? Separating Fact from Fiction
State-by-State Timelines: How Long Landlords Have to Return Your Deposit
How to Write a Security Deposit Dispute Letter That Gets Results
The 60-Second Solution: Send Your Demand Letter Without a Lawyer or Subscription
key_points: Landlords cannot withhold deposits for 'normal wear and tear' or charge arbitrary cleaning fees without documentation in most states.
Most states require landlords to return security deposits within 14 to 30 days or provide an itemized list of deductions.
A formal letter of demand is the critical first step before small claims court and signals that you know your tenant rights.
Deposits-Back.com lets tenants generate a legally sound demand letter for $19.99 in 60 seconds with no signup or subscription required.
output.cta: Don't let your landlord keep what's yours. Create your official security deposit demand letter at Deposits-Back.com now—$19.99, 60 seconds, zero signup.
output.target_audience: Frustrated US renters whose landlord is withholding, not returning, or wrongfully deducting from their security deposit and who need fast, affordable help without legal retainers or subscriptions.
